title: Narrow checkout lifecycle coordination resources
goal: Separate code-path leases, checkout ownership and Git common-dir worktree metadata serialization so non-overlapping code work does not indefinitely block safe checkout archival and cleanup.

approval.note: Source changes and read-only verification are authorized. Runtime deletion or cleanup effects require a fresh exact-target plan and post-readback.

acceptance.assertion: Checkout mutation uses an exact checkout claim plus a short-lived git-metadata claim.
Disjoint source-path work does not automatically block checkout lifecycle when non-overlap is proven.
Mutations of the same Git common directory remain serialized.
Foreign leases, unknown overlaps and broad claims still fail closed.

metadata.source: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1 plan from live 2026-07-14 storage audit

formal_closeout.reason: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1-T004 is verified and PR #222 plus live checkout coordination evidence satisfy all T005 acceptance criteria.

does_not_establish: formal task closure while T004 remains planned
permission to bypass leases or dirty-state guards

live_evidence.finding: grabowski_checkout_archive and grabowski_checkout_retain both rejected the exact clean target because _linked_checkout_coordination included the entire canonical repository path and counted 19 foreign active file leases as blockers although none overlapped the checkout path.
live_evidence.expected_behavior: Serialize only exact checkout and Git common-dir metadata mutations; disjoint source-path leases must not globally block archive or retention.
live_evidence.safety: No foreign lease, process, worktree or dirty state was modified; T042 cleanup remained deferred.

live_evidence.finding: Active disjoint work in the canonical Grabowski checkout no longer blocked the target checkout. The archive receipt recorded zero related resource leases, tasks and processes after the two obsolete owner-local leases were released.
live_evidence.safety: No foreign lease, process, worktree, branch or dirty state was modified.

metadata.readiness_basis: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1-T004 verified by merged Lenskit PR #1017, exact diff-bound review and complete CI/test evidence.

verified_acceptance.resource-model: Checkout lifecycle mutations use the exact checkout scope plus short-lived Git common-directory metadata serialization instead of broad source-tree blocking.
verified_acceptance.nonconflict: Live archive and cleanup evidence proved disjoint source-path work no longer blocks a target checkout when non-overlap is observed.
verified_acceptance.serialization: Git worktree metadata mutation remains serialized for the same common directory.
verified_acceptance.regression: Foreign or broad relevant leases, unknown overlap, dirty state, active tasks and processes still block fail-closed.

completion.outcome: Checkout ownership and Git metadata serialization remain safe while disjoint code-path leases no longer create false global lifecycle blockers.
